input.claim: The national statistics office reported the June unemployment rate at 3.9%.

sources.content: Official national statistics office release: June unemployment rate 3.9%, seasonally adjusted, with methodology notes.
Major wire service reports 3.9%, citing the official release - downstream of the S1 source, not an independent chain.
Economics outlet reports the 3.9% rate, also citing the release.

target.analysis:
Step 1 - Axis-1 resolution. natl-stats-release S1 (95) is the official statistics channel with near-zero error history. wire-service S3 (74) and econ-outlet S4 (63) report the figure, but by citing the release (downstream).

Step 2 - Isnad tracing and independence. The wire service and the economics outlet both trace upstream to the official release - they are echoes, not independent chains. The figure rests on the single authoritative S1 release.

Step 3 - Shudhudh and ilal screens. No conflict and no defect; both screens are clear.

Step 4 - Corroboration (R2 not invoked). Corroboration by an echo of a source is not a second attestation (the same informational-independence principle as record #2). No R2 elevation is available, and none is needed: S1 already anchors V1.

Step 5 - Verdict derivation. R1 anchors on the S1 release to a V1 base (~92). R4 finds the release's precision profile strong and applies a small within-band adjustment, holding the score at 93. R6 attaches a validity horizon: a monthly statistic is valid until the next monthly release, after which it reverts to re-verify. Verdict: sahih (V1, 93) on the S1 anchor alone.

Step 6 - Emission. Clean, authoritative, non-finance; no escalation and no fraud alert.

derivation_trace: R1 (weakest-link anchor): the figure is anchored on the S1 official statistics release (95), which is standalone-sufficient authority, setting the verdict at V1 (~92).
R4 (dabt modifier): the release's precision profile is strong, so a small within-band adjustment holds the score at 93.
R6 (temporal validity): a monthly statistic carries a validity horizon until the next release, after which it reverts to re-verify.
No R2: the wire service and economics outlet cite the release rather than confirm it independently, so they are echoes, not a second chain; S1 alone already supports V1.
